jokes.text: It's a highly sexual experience for people. You'll get no judgments from us.
jokes.setup: Employee explains escape rooms are popular worldwide
jokes.punchline: Character immediately assumes it's sexual and offers no judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - completely misinterprets innocent activity as sexual, shows their warped worldview

dialogue.line: It's a highly sexual experience for people. You'll get no judgments from us.

jokes.text: No. No, no, no. I'm sorry, this is not sexual. It's not sexual. Then I don't know what this is.
jokes.setup: Employee clarifies escape rooms aren't sexual
jokes.punchline: Character genuinely can't understand the concept if it's not sexual
jokes.explanation: Great economy - the complete inability to process non-sexual entertainment is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: No. No, no, no. I'm sorry, this is not sexual. It's not sexual.

dialogue.speaker: Character1
dialogue.line: Then I don't know what this is.

jokes.text: This sounds very nerdy. Is this a nerd thing? It's definitely a nerd thing. I don't do nerd things.

jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal as 'nerd thing' with rapid consensus
jokes.explanation: Standard character moment showing their anti-intellectual bias, but predictable

dialogue.line: This sounds very nerdy.

dialogue.line: Is this a nerd thing?

dialogue.line: It's definitely a nerd thing.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't do nerd things.

jokes.punchline: Group realizes they are her only friends and she went alone

jokes.text: Men don't do things just to do them. Okay? We're busy running the world, providing for our families.
jokes.setup: Dee asks why men need stakes/prizes
jokes.punchline: Dennis's absurdly grandiose justification for needing incentives
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism - positioning doing an escape room as beneath world-running men

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Men don't do things just to do them. Okay? We're busy running the world, providing for our families.

jokes.text: You see, I get out of the room. That means I win the game, the lady here, she takes me out for a steak, and then it becomes sexual.
jokes.setup: Frank suggests steak as prize
jokes.punchline: Frank's bizarre logic chain ending in sex
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the sexual theme while showing Frank's transactional view of relationships

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You see, I get out of the room. That means I win the game, the lady here, she takes me out for a steak, and then it becomes sexual.

jokes.text: Who's that broad? It's Amanda from before. She's just doing a bad British accent.
jokes.setup: Employee starts British accent for game atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately breaks fourth wall calling out the bad acting
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Frank's bluntness cuts through the roleplay pretense

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Who's that broad?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: It's Amanda from before. She's just doing a bad British accent.

jokes.text: Oh, now she's gonna do it. Doth thou... Give me this, 'cause you can't...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, now she's gonna do it. Doth thou... Give me this, 'cause you can't...

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: if you do that accent, then Dee's gonna want to do it, and we hate that.

jokes.text: Frank hasn't been locked up since the nitwit school, so he gets a little tight about it.
jokes.setup: Frank panics about being locked in
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually mentions Frank was in 'nitwit school'

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Frank hasn't been locked up since the nitwit school, so he gets a little tight about it.

jokes.text: Frank, that gum-chewing is insane. The chewing of the gum. It's happening, right in my ear. It's ludicrous. That is the most irritating sound on Earth.
jokes.setup: Frank chews gum loudly
jokes.punchline: Dennis's over-the-top reaction to normal gum chewing
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism - treating minor annoyance as world-ending crisis

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Frank, that gum-chewing is insane. The chewing of the gum. It's happening, right in my ear. It's ludicrous. That is the most irritating sound on Earth.

jokes.text: Well, I spoke too soon. That's the most irritating sound on Earth.
jokes.setup: Dee starts talking about quarterbacking
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately revises his 'most irritating sound' assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with great timing - shows Dennis hates Dee more than any physical irritation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, I spoke too soon. That's the most irritating sound on Earth.

jokes.text: Everybody knows quarterbacks are men. And honestly, like, I don't want to say it out loud, but a female leader? It's just... No. Doesn't sit well, right?
jokes.setup: Dee suggests being quarterback/leader
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis's casually sexist dismissal
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of casual misogyny - they're so terrible they say the quiet part out loud

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Everybody knows quarterbacks are men. And honestly, like, I don't want to say it out loud, but a female leader? It's just... No. Doesn't sit well, right?

jokes.text: Thing is, is like, we had a vote in America. We almost had one, and then America was like, 'Mm...' No women. We couldn't have been more clear about it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about female leadership feeling 'un-American'
jokes.punchline: They interpret election results as America rejecting women leaders
jokes.explanation: Topical political satire delivered through their warped perspective, good specificity

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Thing is, is like, we had a vote in America. We almost had one, and then America was like, 'Mm...' No women. We couldn't have been more clear about it.

jokes.text: Why do you have a lock on the outside of your door? Uh, well, that's... Don't ask.

dialogue.line: Why do you have a lock on the outside of your door?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis

jokes.text: so-so much more relaxed without her in here. That's the move. Both of 'em. I mean, the last thing we need is two women, 'helping us'?

dialogue.speaker: Dee

jokes.punchline: Formal political language to give up leadership he never had
jokes.explanation: Charlie's mock-formal political speech about meaningless positions shows their organizational chaos

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: As your newly elected speaker, I'd like to start by saying I henceforth relinquish any possibility of becoming team leader from myself.

jokes.text: Frank, you have offered to award each one of us with our own 20-ounce sirloin steak, provided you bite yours first.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains their elaborate steak hierarchy
jokes.punchline: The formal terms include Frank getting first bite privilege
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - they've created formal hierarchy even for eating steaks

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Frank, you have offered to award each one of us with our own 20-ounce sirloin steak, provided you bite yours first.

jokes.text: I'd like to take a brief moment to recognize our excellent work as men... navigating these difficult waters with ethics, with grace, with minimal violence. But, above all, without compromise to our masculine identity by any admission of guilt, failure or weakness.
jokes.setup: Charlie's congratulatory speech
jokes.punchline: Praising their 'ethics' and 'minimal violence' while locked in escape room
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - celebrating their 'ethics' and 'grace' when they've been terrible throughout. The masculine fragility is perfectly captured.

dialogue.line: I'd like to take a brief moment to recognize our excellent work as men... navigating these difficult waters with ethics, with grace, with minimal violence. But, above all, without compromise to our masculine identity by any admission of guilt, failure or weakness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, you figured out the only loophole in my carefully curated and well-researched bondage facility.

jokes.text: You took a fall for the boys. That's what a quarterback does. Sacrifices his... or her... body for the team. You really were the quarterback.

jokes.punchline: Sudden reversal praising her leadership after dismissing it earlier

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You took a fall for the boys. That's what a quarterback does. Sacrifices his... or her... body for the team. You really were the quarterback.
